provenance.source_corpus: Enriched Topographic Microsites for Improved Native Grass and Forb Establishment in Reclamation

nodes.name: erosion control blanket, hay, and straw

source_spans.text: The addition of erosion control blanket, hay, and straw to flat sites was just as productive as on topographic microsites.
These amendments increased grass and forb emergence and buffered soil temperature.
Mounds increased first year forb emergence and reduced over winter survival rates for grasses and forbs.
Mounds increased first year forb emergence and reduced over winter survival rates for grasses and forbs.
Mounds increased first year forb emergence and reduced over winter survival rates for grasses and forbs.
Pits were not beneficial for revegetation.
Grass survival was significantly lower in pits at Mattheis than all microsites at the other two sites.
During the 2013 growing season, water content of pits was significantly higher than mounds and flats at Elk Island and mounds at Mattheis.
Near surface soil temperature was significantly affected by topographic microsite, with growing season temperatures in flats and mounds 0.3−2.6oC higher than in pits across sites and years
Near surface soil temperature was significantly affected by topographic microsite, with growing season temperatures in flats and mounds 0.3−2.6oC higher than in pits across sites and years
Treatments that provided ground cover, such as erosion control blanket and straw, generally had 0.5− 2oC lower temperatures than the control and/or manure treatment
Treatments that provided ground cover, such as erosion control blanket and straw, generally had 0.5− 2oC lower temperatures than the control and/or manure treatment
At Mattheis in yr 2, grass emergence with hydrogel and manure was significantly greater than in the control.
Manure increased electrical conductivity at all sites, total organic carbon and total nitrogen at Mattheis, and total organic carbon at Devonian
Manure increased electrical conductivity at all sites, total organic carbon and total nitrogen at Mattheis, and total organic carbon at Devonian
Manure increased electrical conductivity at all sites, total organic carbon and total nitrogen at Mattheis, and total organic carbon at Devonian
Manure increased electrical conductivity at all sites, total organic carbon and total nitrogen at Mattheis, and total organic carbon at Devonian
Straw had significantly less new grass emergence than other treatments except hay and was not favorable to blue grama.
